evidence.scientific_clarity: Grouped bar chart clearly shows OursModel winning on all 5 benchmarks with error bars; brief's claim (highlight overall winner, single-column ready) fully met.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' are set; font sizes 7-8pt are reasonable; however the embedded font is DejaVuSans (matplotlib default) rather than a preferred publication font.
evidence.palette: Wong (2011) colorblind-safe palette used (grey, sky-blue, orange, green); green reserved for the winning method (directional use); no red/green direction-confusion issue.
evidence.layout_density: Figure is 3.5x2.6 in (single-column); y-range is dynamically computed avoiding forced zero; 2-column legend fits inside plot area; tight_layout(pad=0.4) eliminates dead space.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption in bold names the winner and gives a +6.4 pp numerical claim; legend is upper-left inside axes; caption matches figure content; no title (caption-first); synthetic-data caveat included.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF confirmed to contain BT/ET text operators (selectable text) and FontFile2 (embedded TrueType); script explicitly sets pdf.fonttype=42.
evidence.file_format: All five required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (57 KB), figure.png (94 KB), figure.svg (287 KB), caption.tex (429 B), gen_figure.py (3.4 KB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states what is shown (4 methods, 5 benchmarks, error bars = std over 5 seeds), names the winning method in bold, provides a specific quantitative finding (+6.4 pp over strongest baseline), and discloses synthetic data.

overall_strengths: Full vector pipeline: pdf.fonttype=42 + svg.fonttype='none' ensure editable text in both PDF and SVG outputs.
Colorblind-safe Wong palette with hatch patterns for B&W accessibility, and a quantified caption (+6.4 pp) that meets reviewer expectations.
overall_weaknesses: Font is DejaVuSans (matplotlib default) rather than a publication-standard sans-serif (e.g., Helvetica, Arial, or Latin Modern Sans); a reviewer may flag this.
data.json is not present in the working directory, so the +6.4 pp caption claim cannot be independently verified against the source data.
